title: malacothamnus fasciculatus subsp. catalinensis
intro: Malacothamnus fasciculatus subsp. catalinensis, also known as Catalina bushmallow or Catalina island bushmallow, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mallow family Malvaceae. This plant is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. The health benefits of Malacothamnus fasciculatus subsp. catalinensis include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to help with digestive issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to reap its medicinal benefits.
